title: adenophorus tamariscinus var. montanus
intro: Adenophorus tamariscinus var. montanus, also known as Kōka or Montane Adenophorus, is a plant species native to Hawaii, belonging to the club moss family and is used as a medicinal plant. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, treat skin conditions, and alleviate respiratory issues, among others. In herbal preparations, Kōka is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, making it a valuable resource for traditional Hawaiian medicine.
